title: Introduction to Machine Learning
author: John Smith
content: Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence that enables systems to learn and improve from experience without being explicitly programmed. It focuses on developing computer programs that can access data and use it to learn for themselves.

title: Deep Learning Fundamentals
author: Jane Doe
content: Deep learning is a subset of machine learning based on artificial neural networks with multiple input, output, and hidden layers. These networks attempt to simulate the behavior of the human brain in processing data for decision making.

title: Natural Language Processing
author: Robert Johnson
content: Natural Language Processing (NLP) is a branch of AI that helps computers understand, interpret and manipulate human language. NLP draws from linguistics and computer science to bridge the gap between human communication and computer understanding.

title: Computer Vision Basics
author: Emily Chen
content: Computer vision is an interdisciplinary field that deals with how computers can gain high-level understanding from digital images or videos. It seeks to automate tasks that the human visual system can do, like identifying objects and faces.

title: Reinforcement Learning
author: Michael Brown
content: Reinforcement learning is an area of machine learning concerned with how intelligent agents take actions to maximize cumulative reward. It differs from supervised learning in that correct input/output pairs need not be presented.
